# Elm Material Design SVG Icons (from pictogrammers.com)

I _love_ me some SVG icons.

You know what I love even more? SVG icons that you can compose.

"Compose?" you ask. "What's that mean?"

Well, what I mean by that is you can take an icon and add different types of
decorations to it, like cogs and eyes and map markers.


## Table of contents

- [Using the icons](#using-the-icons)
- [Coloring the icons](#coloring-the-icons)
- [Coloring the decorations](#coloring-the-decorations)
- [Gratitude](#gratitude)

## Using the icons

First, find the base icon you want to use. They're listed and shown in the
[Mdi.Icons](./Mdi-Icons) module.

Let's say you want to use a hot air balloon icon. To render it, just use the
[`toSvg`](Mdi#toSvg) function.

    Mdi.Icons.airballoon
        |> Mdi.toSvg

![airballoon](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

You want the outline version?

    Mdi.Icons.airballoon
        |> Mdi.outline
        |> Mdi.toSvg

![airballoon-outline](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Want to put a heart on it?

    Mdi.Icons.airballoon
        |> Mdi.outline
        |> Mdi.heart
        |> Mdi.toSvg

![airballoon-outline+heart](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

## Coloring the icons

The icons are set to have a fill color that is the current color of the text
rendering context.

The following code will render a green hot air balloon.

    div [ style "color" "green" ]
        [ Mdi.Icons.airballoon |> Mdi.toSvg ]

![airballoon](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

## Coloring the decorations

By default, the decorations will be the same color as the icon.

    div [ style "color" "green" ]
        [ Mdi.Icons.airballoon |> Mdi.heart |> Mdi.toSvg ]

![airballoon-outline+heart](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

You can use CSS to target the color of the decoration by setting the `color`
property to whatever you want.

```css
<style>
  .overlay-circle { color: red; }
</style>
```

![airballoon-outline+heart](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

You can also use the [`decorationColor`](./Mdi#decorationColor) function. (I'm 
not a fan of this, so much, but I'm like _that_ person that doesn't mind CSS.)

    div [ style "color" "green" ]
        [ Mdi.Icons.airballoon 
            |> Mdi.heart 
            |> Mdi.decorationColor "hsl(0, 100%, 50%)"
            |> Mdi.toSvg 
        ]

## Gratitude

The base icons come from Pictogrammer's [Material Design
Icons](https://pictogrammers.com/library/mdi/) library. Lots of 🫶 to them for
the artwork.

The overlays are inspired by the MDI icon styles, but have been customized to
fit the SVG clip paths used by this library.
